mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate directly reduce the discount rate applied to future equity cash flows, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window, or positive 20-day returns occur in fewer than 50% of rate-decline instances.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month increase in the unemployment rate exceeding 0.2 percentage points signals deteriorating labor market conditions that immediately suppress corporate earnings expectations, driving negative S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: Forward returns are positive in more than 65% of instances where the unemployment rate crosses the 0.2 percentage point threshold.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous deceleration in real GDP growth and acceleration in consumer price inflation creates a stagflationary environment that compresses profit margins and equity valuations, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to predict negative returns when GDP growth falls below 1% annualized while CPI rises above 3% annualized.

mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month increases in the index of industrial production reflect strengthening manufacturing output and supply-side capacity utilization, which generates positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Short-term returns remain flat or negative despite industrial production growth exceeding 0.5% month-over-month for three consecutive months.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained month-over-month growth in total nonfarm payrolls indicates robust labor demand and consumer spending capacity, which directly supports corporate revenue growth and yields positive S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: Payroll growth above 150,000 per month correlates with negative 5-day forward returns in more than 60% of observations over a 5-year sample.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month decline in retail sales exceeding 2% signals a sharp contraction in consumer demand that immediately erodes earnings forecasts, driving negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The S&P 500 records positive 20-day returns in more than 70% of months where retail sales drop by more than 2%.

mechanism_narrative: The combination of an elevated federal funds rate and persistently high consumer price inflation forces aggressive monetary tightening expectations, which increases discount rates and compresses equity multiples, resulting in negative S&P 500 forward returns over 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Equity returns remain positive when the federal funds rate exceeds 4% and CPI inflation remains above 3% for consecutive quarters.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent expansion in industrial production and nonfarm payrolls confirms broad-based economic acceleration that lifts corporate profit margins and equity valuations, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over 5 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint signal produces negative forward returns in more than 55% of instances when both series register positive month-over-month growth.
